svg.text: RISC-V Base and Compressed Instruction Formats
32-bit Base Instruction Formats opcode in [6:0] funct3 in [14:12]
B and J immediate bits are scrambled across the encoding to keep the sign bit (MSB) always at inst[31], minimizing sign-extension hardware.
16-bit Compressed (RVC) Formats op in [1:0] (quadrant) funct3/funct4 in upper bits
Primed registers (rd', rs1', rs2') address only x8-x15 / f8-f15 (3-bit field). op [1:0] identifies the RVC quadrant (00, 01, 10).
integer register (rs1, rs2, rd and primed variants)
Figure N: 32-bit base formats (R/I/S/B/U/J) and 16-bit compressed formats (CR/CI/CSS/CIW/CL/CS/CB/CJ).
The RVC expander upstream of the decoder reconstitutes 16-bit instructions into their 32-bit equivalents before the main decode stage.
